prompt.content: A student says, “Parallel lines are lines that go in the same direction.” Which revision makes the definition mathematically precise?

choices.content: Parallel lines are lines that are always the same length.
Parallel lines are lines in the same plane that do not intersect.
Parallel lines are lines that intersect to form equal angles.
Parallel lines are lines that share exactly one point.

steps.content: Parallel lines must be in the same plane and must not intersect. “Go in the same direction” is informal and incomplete.
